beats.narration_text: Shalom, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. The spec-sheet math promised 367 milliseconds. The first run on real hardware took 1,200 — and both numbers were right.

props.command: Why did my model predict 367 ms latency but measure 1,200 ms on real hardware — and how do I close that gap?
output: SIMD kernels + memory spill + int8 → 1,200 ms → 45 ms

beats.narration_text: The book's latency waterfall: three hundred sixty-seven milliseconds predicted, twelve hundred on first deployment, forty-five after profiling. The gap between prediction and reality is the story.

The spec-sheet calculation used ideal kernel throughput — 367 milliseconds. The first hardware run measured 1,200. The 100-millisecond target line sits far below both. Three named causes put you there, and you can fix them one at a time.

First fix: swap reference kernels for SIMD-optimized kernels. Reference C code calls one multiply per instruction. SIMD packs four int8 values into one register and runs them in a single clock. The bar drops from 1,200 to roughly 400 milliseconds.

Second fix: stop spilling activations to external flash. When intermediate tensors overflow SRAM, the model fetches them from slow external memory on every access. Move those activations onto-chip and the bar falls from 400 to roughly 200 milliseconds.

Third fix: quantize weights and activations to int8. Float32 inference uses four bytes per value; int8 uses one. Compute narrows, memory bandwidth narrows, and the bar drops from 200 to 45 milliseconds — landing well under the 100-millisecond target line.

Your turn. Paste this into Claude: My model's profiler shows total-ms milliseconds inference time. The main bottleneck is layer-type at bottleneck-ms milliseconds. Walk me through: one, how to tell if it's memory-bound versus compute-bound; two, what reference versus SIMD kernels means and how to switch; three, what activation spilling is; four, expected speedup from int8 on my processor chip. Swap in your real profiler numbers — model, layer type, chip — and get a targeted optimization plan.

props.command: My model's profiler shows [total_ms] ms inference time. The main bottleneck is [layer_type] at [bottleneck_ms] ms. Walk me through: (1) how to tell if it's memory-bound vs compute-bound, (2) what reference vs SIMD kernels means and how to switch, (3) what activation spilling is, (4) expected speedup from int8 on my [processor] chip.

props.title: Predicted 367 ms. Measured 1,200.
props.subline: embedded ai · liam, in for bear
